crawl.definition: The act of moving slowly on hands and knees etc, or with frequent stops.

creator.definition: Something or someone which creates or makes something.

credibility.definition: Reputation impacting one's ability to be believed.

creep.definition: To move slowly with the abdomen close to the ground.

crown.definition: A royal, imperial or princely headdress; a diadem.

crude.definition: Any substance in its natural state.

crush.definition: A violent collision or compression; a crash; destruction; ruin.

crystal.definition: A solid composed of an array of atoms or molecules possessing long-range order and arranged in a pattern which is periodic in three dimensions.

cult.definition: A group or sect of people with a deviant religious, philosophical or cultural identity, often existing on the margins of society or exploitative towards its members.

curiosity.definition: (uncountable) Inquisitiveness; the tendency to ask and learn about things by asking questions, investigating, or exploring.

custody.definition: The legal right to take care of something or somebody, especially children.

cutting.definition: (heading) To incise, to cut into the surface of something.

cynical.definition: Of or relating to the belief that human actions are motivated only or primarily by base desires or selfishness.

dam.definition: A structure placed across a flowing body of water to stop the flow or part of the flow, generally for purposes such as retaining or diverting some of the water or retarding the release of accumulated water to avoid abrupt flooding.

damaging.definition: To impair the soundness, goodness, or value of; to harm or cause destruction.

dawn.definition: The morning twilight period immediately before sunrise.

debris.definition: Rubble, wreckage, scattered remains of something destroyed.

debut.definition: A performer's first performance to the public, in sport, the arts or some other area.

decisive.definition: Having the power or quality of deciding a question or controversy; putting an end to contest or controversy; final; conclusive.

declaration.definition: A written or oral indication of a fact, opinion, or belief.

dedicated.definition: To set apart for a deity or for religious purposes; consecrate.

dedication.definition: The act of dedicating or the state of being dedicated.

deed.definition: An action or act; something that is done.

deem.definition: An opinion, a judgment, a surmise.

default.definition: The condition of failing to meet an obligation.

defensive.definition: A means, attitude or position of defense.

deficit.definition: Deficiency in amount or quality; a falling short; lack.

delegate.definition: A person authorized to act as representative for another; a deputy

delicate.definition: A delicate item of clothing, especially underwear or lingerie.

denounce.definition: To make known in a formal manner; to proclaim; to announce; to declare.

density.definition: A measure of the mass of matter contained by a unit volume.

dependence.definition: The state of being dependent, of relying upon another.

depict.definition: To render a representation of something, using words, sounds, images, or other means.

deployment.definition: An arrangement or classification of things.

deprive.definition: To take something away from (someone) and keep it away; to deny someone something.

deputy.definition: One appointed as the substitute of another, and empowered to act for them, in their name or their behalf; a substitute in office

descend.definition: To pass from a higher to a lower place; to move downwards; to come or go down in any way, for example by falling, flowing, walking, climbing etc.

descent.definition: An instance of descending; act of coming down.

designate.definition: To mark out and make known; to point out; to indicate; to show; to distinguish by marks or description

desirable.definition: A thing that people want; something that is desirable.

desktop.definition: The top surface of a desk.

detain.definition: To keep someone from proceeding by holding them back or making claims on their attention.

detection.definition: The act of detecting or sensing something; discovering something that was hidden or disguised.

detention.definition: The act of detaining or the state of being detained.

deteriorate.definition: To make worse; to make inferior in quality or value; to impair.

devastate.definition: To ruin many or all things over a large area, such as most or all buildings of a city, or cities of a region, or trees of a forest.

devise.definition: The act of leaving real property in a will.

diagnose.definition: To determine which disease is causing a sick person's signs and symptoms; to find the diagnosis.

diagnosis.definition: To determine which disease is causing a sick person's signs and symptoms; to find the diagnosis.

dictator.definition: A totalitarian leader of a country, nation, or government.

differentiate.definition: Something that has been differentiated or stratified.

dignity.definition: The state of being dignified or worthy of esteem: elevation of mind or character.

dilemma.definition: A circumstance in which a choice must be made between two or more alternatives that seem equally undesirable.

dimension.definition: A single aspect of a given thing.

dip.definition: A lower section of a road or geological feature.

diplomat.definition: A person, such as an ambassador, who is accredited to represent a government officially in its relations with other governments or international organisations

diplomatic.definition: The science of diplomas, or the art of deciphering ancient writings and determining their age, authenticity, etc.; paleography.

directory.definition: A list of names, addresses etc, of specific classes of people or organizations, often in alphabetical order or in some classification.

disastrous.definition: Of the nature of a disaster; calamitous.

discharge.definition: Pus or exudate (other than blood) from a wound or orifice, usually due to infection or pathology.

discretion.definition: The quality of being discreet or circumspect.

discrimination.definition: Discernment, the act of discriminating, discerning, distinguishing, noting or perceiving differences between things, with intent to understand rightly and make correct decisions.

dismissal.definition: The act of sending someone away.

displace.definition: To put out of place; to disarrange.

disposal.definition: An arrangement, categorization or classification of things.

dispose.definition: The disposal or management of something.

dispute.definition: An argument or disagreement, a failure to agree.

disrupt.definition: To throw into confusion or disorder.

disruption.definition: An interruption to the regular flow or sequence of something.

dissolve.definition: A form of film punctuation in which there is a gradual transition from one scene to the next

distinction.definition: That which distinguishes; a single occurrence of a determining factor or feature, the fact of being divided; separation, discrimination.

distinctive.definition: A distinctive thing: a quality or property permitting distinguishing; a characteristic.

distort.definition: To bring something out of shape, to misshape.

disturbing.definition: To confuse a quiet, constant state or a calm, continuous flow, in particular: thoughts, actions or liquids.

divert.definition: To turn aside from a course.

divine.definition: One skilled in divinity; a theologian.

doctrine.definition: A belief or tenet, especially about philosophical or theological matters.

documentation.definition: Something transposed from a thought to a document; the written account of an idea.
